title: tephrosia lupinifolia
intro: Tephrosia lupinifolia, also known as Lupine Bush or Devil's Thorn, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions of the world, characterized by its thorny stems and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of alkaloids like lupinifolin and lupanine. The health benefits of Tephrosia lupinifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds, infections, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity and side effects.
